addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Pace: charge-folds-readiness-wait (₢BBAAt) [rough]
Heat: ₣BB

[0] rough (basis: ff29871)
    Silks: charge-folds-readiness-wait

    ## Character
    
    Convert post-charge readiness-wait from a theurge-side matcher into a
    nameplate-borne fact, with the wait folded into the charge tabtarget itself.
    After charge returns, the case is ready — theurge holds no readiness concern.
    
    ## Scope
    
    - Add `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` to the nameplate regime
      (tadmor=0, srjcl/pluml=30 currently).
    - Charge tabtarget consults the fact and waits internally before returning.
    - Theurge: `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` matcher and post-charge wait block
      both removed.
    - Three colluding pieces collapsed: bash const
      `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`, Rust mirror
      `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS`, Rust matcher
      `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay`.
    
    ## Wrap criterion
    
    - Charge honors per-nameplate readiness delay; theurge has no readiness code.
    - tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution.
    
    ## Cross-pace dependencies
    
    - BBAAs landed (fixture lifecycle is the right shape for this hand-off).
    
    ## Out of scope
    
    - Service-readiness probe contract (poll port-open or curl health endpoint
      until truly ready). Bigger contract upgrade; blind-sleep-by-nameplate-fact
      is sufficient. Surface as itch.
    
    ## References
    
    - Discovery clue: grep `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL` to find the locality the new field
      needs plumbing through — per-nameplate `.rbk/*/rbrn.env`, `rbrn_regime.sh`,
      `rbrn_cli.sh`, `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`, and runtime consumers.
    - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — current post-charge wait block in theurge
      orchestration.
    - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs::rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` —
      current matcher.
    - `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — bash const definition.

Heat: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) [racing]
Paddock: .claude/jjm/jjp_uBuB.md

Next: rbw-tP-first-run (₢BBAAX) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Collision-aware diagnostic
  
  Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  - BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

ed898867  ₢BBABE    Fix shape A: sentry takes ownership of its default route. Cross-platform empirical work confirmed BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced a latent fragility — both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x ignore compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` for default-route selection, picking the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (= enclave). Pre-rename the special `services.networks.default` path made compose pick the right gateway by accident; BBABC removed that luck. Implementation: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink-interface discovery now computes the uplink gateway as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention; verified arithmetic for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Compute uses POSIX sh arithmetic (script is `#!/bin/sh`): IFS=.read into octets via heredoc, bitwise mask via `0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1<<host_bits)-1)`, gateway = network_address + 1. Spec deltas extend the existing don't-trust-Docker discipline (already in place for IP-based interface naming, RBSSS step 2) to default-route ownership: RBS0's at_sentry_container narrative gains a sentence pointing at the `priority`-not-honored finding; RBSSS step 2 retitled `Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing` with two new sub-bullets. Compose YAML cleanup: removed the broken `priority: 1000` / `priority: 500` keys (empirically inert); rewrote the misleading topology header that claimed `eth0=transit priority:1000` (eth0 is empirically enclave on both platforms — that comment was wrong since the IP-discovery requirement was added); new comment names the engine versions tested. Bottle vessel unchanged (no rebuild needed); sentry vessel needs re-kludge since rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image at build time per RBS0:2416. Excludes Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh which is uncommitted from another officium per multi-officium discipline.

Both vessels (sentry rbev-sentry-deb-tether, bottle rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether) now built locally on this mac and ready to charge for cross-platform characterization of BBABC default-route regression.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; this pace exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  Post-BBABB landing: this pace may run concurrently with BBABA on a different machine when both stations carry distinct BURS_TINCTURE values. Tincture composition makes RBDC-derived names disjoint per station; the remaining collision surface is operator error (same tincture on both stations).
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Collision-aware diagnostic
  
  Any GCP "resource already exists" or equivalent name-collision error during the run produces a diagnostic that names BURS_TINCTURE and the colliding term, with a hypothesis pointing at peer-station tincture overlap. Distinguishes tincture-composition collision from transient GCP race.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  - BBABB landed (BURS_TINCTURE composition; required for the parallel-station property)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

ed898867  ₢BBABE    Fix shape A: sentry takes ownership of its default route. Cross-platform empirical work confirmed BBABC's `default → transit` rename surfaced a latent fragility — both linux Docker engine 28.x AND mac Docker Desktop 28.x ignore compose `services.<svc>.networks.<net>.priority` for default-route selection, picking the alphabetically-first attached network's gateway (= enclave). Pre-rename the special `services.networks.default` path made compose pick the right gateway by accident; BBABC removed that luck. Implementation: `rbjs_sentry.sh` after RBJp1 uplink-interface discovery now computes the uplink gateway as the first usable IP of the uplink subnet (Docker bridge convention; verified arithmetic for /16 → 172.19.0.1, /24 → 10.242.0.1) and runs `ip route replace default via <gw> dev ${RBJ_UPLINK_IF}`. Compute uses POSIX sh arithmetic (script is `#!/bin/sh`): IFS=.read into octets via heredoc, bitwise mask via `0xFFFFFFFF ^ ((1<<host_bits)-1)`, gateway = network_address + 1. Spec deltas extend the existing don't-trust-Docker discipline (already in place for IP-based interface naming, RBSSS step 2) to default-route ownership: RBS0's at_sentry_container narrative gains a sentence pointing at the `priority`-not-honored finding; RBSSS step 2 retitled `Sentry discovers interfaces and takes ownership of routing` with two new sub-bullets. Compose YAML cleanup: removed the broken `priority: 1000` / `priority: 500` keys (empirically inert); rewrote the misleading topology header that claimed `eth0=transit priority:1000` (eth0 is empirically enclave on both platforms — that comment was wrong since the IP-discovery requirement was added); new comment names the engine versions tested. Bottle vessel unchanged (no rebuild needed); sentry vessel needs re-kludge since rbjs_sentry.sh is baked into the image at build time per RBS0:2416. Excludes Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh which is uncommitted from another officium per multi-officium discipline.

content.text:
## Mount: ₢BBAAt (charge-folds-readiness-wait)

**Pace goal**: Move the post-charge readiness wait off the theurge and into the charge tabtarget itself, sourced from a new nameplate-borne fact (`RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`). After charge returns, the case is ready — theurge holds no readiness concern.

**Heat**: ₣BB rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (racing, 45/57)

### Three colluding pieces to collapse
1. Bash const `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` in `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh`
2. Rust mirror `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS`
3. Rust matcher `rbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` + the post-charge wait block in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs`

### Proposed approach
- **Plumb the fact**: Add `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` through the per-nameplate regime chain — `.rbk/*/rbrn.env` (tadmor=0, srjcl=30, pluml=30), `rbrn_regime.sh`, `rbrn_cli.sh`, `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`.
- **Fold the wait into charge**: The charge tabtarget consults the rendered fact and sleeps before returning.
- **Excise theurge readiness code**: Remove the matcher, the Rust delay constant, the post-charge wait block, and the bash const.
- **Verify**: Fixture execution against tadmor/srjcl/pluml.

### Wrap criterion
Charge honors per-nameplate readiness delay; theurge has no readiness code; tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass.

**Name**: `charge-folds-readiness-wait` fits the docket well — no rename needed.

**Execution**: Sequential opus — touches regime plumbing across bash + Rust + spec, with a fixture verification at the end. Some judgment needed for the new field's render path and clean removal of the colluding triple; not parallelizable.

Proceed?

message.content: Orient yourself: do not change filesystem, but do think through all work needed.  We have to wait for a parallel chat to finish to change the filesystem.

# Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {

RBRN_DESCRIPTION="Local PlantUML Server for diagram rendering"
RBRN_RUNTIME=docker

# Vessel Source Configuration (GAR vessels)
RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=rbev-sentry-deb-tether
RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=rbev-bottle-plantuml
RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=c260506180953-r260506180957
RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=b260506190106-r260506190109

# Port Service Configuration
RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=8001
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=8080

# Network Uplink Configuration
RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=10000
RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=disabled
RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=disabled
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS=0.0.0.0/0
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=localhost

# Network Configuration
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=10.242.1.0
RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=24
RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=10.242.1.2
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=10.242.1.3

# eof

20

24	test -z "${ZRBRN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBRN_SOURCED=1

27	######################################################################

30	zrbrn_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn already kindled"
32	
33	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
34	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
35	
36	  # Enroll all RBRN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
37	
38	  buv_regime_enroll RBRN
39	
40	  buv_group_enroll "Core Service Identity"
41	  buv_xname_enroll   RBRN_MONIKER                 2   12  "Unique identifier for Crucible"
42	  buv_string_enroll  RBRN_DESCRIPTION              0  120  "Human-readable description"
43	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_RUNTIME                              "Container runtime: docker or podman" \
44	                     docker podman
45	
46	  buv_group_enroll "Container Image Configuration"
47	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Sentry Image"
48	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
49	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Sentry Image"
50	  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Bottle Image"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Entry Service Configuration"
53	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE                           "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled" \
54	                     disabled enabled
55	  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE  enabled
56	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION               "External port on Transit Network"
57	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE                   "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"
58	
59	  buv_group_enroll "Enclave Network Configuration"
60	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP                      "Base IPv4 for enclave network"
61	  buv_decimal_enroll RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK          8   30  "Network mask width (8-30)"
62	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP                    "IP address for Sentry Container"
63	  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP                    "IP address for Bottle Container"
64	
65	  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Core"
66	  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN                      "Minimum port for outbound connections"
67	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE                      "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
68	                     disabled global allowlist
69	  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE                   "IP access mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
70	                     disabled global allowlist

80	  # Guard against unexpected RBRN_ variables not in enrollment
81	  buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_

83	  # Lock all enrolled RBRN_ variables against mutation
84	  buv_lock RBRN
85	
86	  readonly ZRBRN_KINDLED=1
87	}
88	
89	zrbrn_sentinel() {
90	  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
91	}
92	
93	# Enforce all RBRN enrollment validations and custom format checks
94	zrbrn_enforce() {
95	  zrbrn_sentinel
96	
97	  buv_vet RBRN
98	
99	  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
100	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
101	  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
102	
103	  # Cross-port check (entry ports must be less than uplink port min)
104	  if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
105	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
106	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
107	    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
108	      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
109	  fi
110	
111	  # Build docker env args array from validated values
112	  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
113	  buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
114	}
115	
116	######################################################################
117	# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
118	
119	# List available nameplate monikers as space-separated tokens
120	# Prerequisite: RBCC sourced (needs RBBC_dot_dir, RBCC_rbrn_file)
121	rbrn_list_capture() {
122	  zrbcc_sentinel
123	
124	  local z_result=""
125	  local z_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
126	  local z_i=""
127	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
128	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
129	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
130	    local z_moniker="${z_dir##*/}"
131	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_moniker}"
132	  done
133	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
134	  echo "${z_result}"
135	}
136	
137	######################################################################
138	# Cross-Nameplate Functions
139	#
140	# rbrn_preflight:  Requires RBCC kindled (called from CLI rbrn_audit)
141	
142	# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
143	zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
144	  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
145	  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
146	  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
147	}
148	
149	# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
150	# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
151	zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
152	  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
153	  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
154	  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
155	  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
156	  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
157	    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
158	  fi
159	}
160	
161	# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
162	# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
163	rbrn_preflight() {
164	  zrbcc_sentinel
165	
166	  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via isolation subshells
167	  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
168	  local z_data_lines=()
169	  local z_nf_i=""
170	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
171	    test -f "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}" || continue
172	    local z_line
173	    z_line=$(
174	      bash -c '
175	        source "$1" || exit 1
176	        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
177	      ' _ "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
178	    ) || buc_die "Preflight isolation failed for: ${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
179	    z_data_lines+=("${z_line}")
180	  done
181	
182	  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
183	  local z_ws_port_keys=()
184	  local z_ws_port_vals=()
185	  local z_enc_port_keys=()
186	  local z_enc_port_vals=()
187	  local z_ip_keys=()
188	  local z_ip_vals=()
189	  local z_net_starts=()
190	  local z_net_ends=()
191	  local z_net_owners=()
192	
193	  local z_mon=""
194	  local z_entry=""
195	  local z_ws=""
196	  local z_enc=""
197	  local z_base=""
198	  local z_mask=""
199	  local z_sentry=""
200	  local z_bottle=""
201	  for z_nf_i in "${!z_data_lines[@]}"; do
202	    IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle <<< "${z_data_lines[$z_nf_i]}" \
203	      || buc_die "Failed to parse nameplate data line"
204	    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue
205	
206	    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
207	    if test "${z_entry}" = "enabled"; then
208	      local z_i
209	      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
210	        if test "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_ws}"; then
211	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
212	        fi
213	      done
214	      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
215	      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
216	
217	      # Enclave ports are scoped to their Docker network — key on base_ip:port
218	      local z_enc_key="${z_base}:${z_enc}"
219	      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
220	        if test "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_enc_key}"; then
221	          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} on network ${z_base} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
222	        fi
223	      done
224	      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc_key}")
225	      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
226	    fi
227	
228	    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
229	    local z_j
230	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
231	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_sentry}"; then
232	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
233	      fi
234	    done
235	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
236	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")
237	
238	    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
239	      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_bottle}"; then
240	        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
241	      fi
242	    done
243	    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
244	    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")
245	
246	    # Subnet non-overlap
247	    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
248	    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
249	    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
250	    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
251	    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))
252	
253	    local z_k
254	    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
255	      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
256	        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
257	      fi
258	    done
259	    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
260	    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
261	    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")
262	
263	  done
264	}
265	
266	# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2024 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Regime Nameplate - Validator Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBRN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBRN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbrn_*)

zrbrn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all RBRN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll RBRN

  buv_group_enroll "Core Service Identity"
  buv_xname_enroll   RBRN_MONIKER                 2   12  "Unique identifier for Crucible"
  buv_string_enroll  RBRN_DESCRIPTION              0  120  "Human-readable description"
  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_RUNTIME                              "Container runtime: docker or podman" \
                     docker podman

  buv_group_enroll "Container Image Configuration"
  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Sentry Image"
  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL            1  128  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Sentry Image"
  buv_fqin_enroll    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK      1  128  "Hallmark tag for Bottle Image"

  buv_group_enroll "Entry Service Configuration"
  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE                           "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled" \
                     disabled enabled
  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE  enabled
  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION               "External port on Transit Network"
  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE                   "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"

  buv_group_enroll "Enclave Network Configuration"
  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP                      "Base IPv4 for enclave network"
  buv_decimal_enroll RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK          8   30  "Network mask width (8-30)"
  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP                    "IP address for Sentry Container"
  buv_ipv4_enroll    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP                    "IP address for Bottle Container"

  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Core"
  buv_port_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN                      "Minimum port for outbound connections"
  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE                      "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
                     disabled global allowlist
  buv_enum_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE                   "IP access mode: disabled, global, or allowlist" \
                     disabled global allowlist

  buv_group_enroll "Uplink DNS Allowlist"
  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE  allowlist
  buv_list_domain_enroll RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS           "Allowed DNS domains"

  buv_group_enroll "Uplink Access Allowlist"
  buv_gate_enroll    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE  allowlist
  buv_list_cidr_enroll   RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS             "Allowed CIDR ranges"

  # Guard against unexpected RBRN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_

  # Lock all enrolled RBRN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock RBRN

  readonly ZRBRN_KINDLED=1
}

zrbrn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all RBRN enrollment validations and custom format checks
zrbrn_enforce() {
  zrbrn_sentinel

  buv_vet RBRN

  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"

  # Cross-port check (entry ports must be less than uplink port min)
  if test "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" = "enabled"; then
    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
    test "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}" -lt "${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}" || \
      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
  fi

  # Build docker env args array from validated values
  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
  buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (rbrn_*)

# List available nameplate monikers as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: RBCC sourced (needs RBBC_dot_dir, RBCC_rbrn_file)
rbrn_list_capture() {
  zrbcc_sentinel

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_moniker="${z_dir##*/}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_moniker}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

######################################################################
# Cross-Nameplate Functions
#
# rbrn_preflight:  Requires RBCC kindled (called from CLI rbrn_audit)

# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
}

# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
  fi
}

# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
rbrn_preflight() {
  zrbcc_sentinel

  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via isolation subshells
  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBBC_dot_dir}/"*"/${RBCC_rbrn_file}")
  local z_data_lines=()
  local z_nf_i=""
  for z_nf_i in "${!z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}" || continue
    local z_line
    z_line=$(
      bash -c '
        source "$1" || exit 1
        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
      ' _ "${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
    ) || buc_die "Preflight isolation failed for: ${z_nameplate_files[$z_nf_i]}"
    z_data_lines+=("${z_line}")
  done

  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
  local z_ws_port_keys=()
  local z_ws_port_vals=()
  local z_enc_port_keys=()
  local z_enc_port_vals=()
  local z_ip_keys=()
  local z_ip_vals=()
  local z_net_starts=()
  local z_net_ends=()
  local z_net_owners=()

  local z_mon=""
  local z_entry=""
  local z_ws=""
  local z_enc=""
  local z_base=""
  local z_mask=""
  local z_sentry=""
  local z_bottle=""
  for z_nf_i in "${!z_data_lines[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle <<< "${z_data_lines[$z_nf_i]}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to parse nameplate data line"
    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue

    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
    if test "${z_entry}" = "enabled"; then
      local z_i
      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
        if test "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_ws}"; then
          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
        fi
      done
      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")

      # Enclave ports are scoped to their Docker network — key on base_ip:port
      local z_enc_key="${z_base}:${z_enc}"
      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
        if test "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" = "${z_enc_key}"; then
          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} on network ${z_base} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
        fi
      done
      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc_key}")
      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
    fi

    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
    local z_j
    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_sentry}"; then
        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
      fi
    done
    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")

    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
      if test "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" = "${z_bottle}"; then
        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
      fi
    done
    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")

    # Subnet non-overlap
    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))

    local z_k
    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
      fi
    done
    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")

  done
}

file.content:
rbob_charge() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
  fi

  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
  rbrn_preflight

  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
  fi

  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. The
  # collision scan refuses on any foreign compose project whose network
  # bears our moniker (catches pre-prefix-era residue independent of the
  # current run's project name); the subnet scan catches non-compose
  # networks that bypass label-based detection.
  zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision
  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict

  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait

  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
rbob_charged_predicate() {

content.content:
80	pub struct rbtdre_Section {
81	    pub name: &'static str,
82	    pub cases: &'static [rbtdre_Case],
83	}
84	
85	/// A complete fixture definition — name, disposition, optional setup/teardown
86	/// hooks, and section array. All fixture-level metadata in one place; replaces
87	/// the prior side-channel matchers (sections_for_fixture, fixture_disposition,
88	/// needs_charge, needs_readiness_delay) keyed off the fixture name string.
89	///
90	/// `setup` runs before any cases. Failure aborts the fixture; cases do not run;
91	/// `teardown` still runs (finally-shaped) for any partial-state cleanup.
92	///
93	/// `teardown` runs unconditionally after cases regardless of case verdicts or
94	/// setup outcome. Errors are surfaced as warnings — teardown is best-effort.

85	
86	/// Setup hook for crucible fixtures: charge → CrucibleActive assertion →
87	/// readiness delay (for service-bearing nameplates). Reads ctx from thread-
88	/// local. Returns Err on any step failure; engine surfaces this as a fixture
89	/// failure and runs teardown anyway.
90	///
91	/// Readiness-delay handling currently consults a hardcoded service-fixture
92	/// set; pace ₢BBAAt migrates the delay to a per-nameplate fact
93	/// (`RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC`) read by the charge tabtarget itself,
94	/// at which point this function loses the wait entirely.
95	pub fn rbtdrc_charge_crucible() -> Result<(), String> {
96	    RBTDRC_CTX.with(|c| {
97	        let mut opt = c.borrow_mut();
98	        let ctx = opt
99	            .as_mut()
100	            .ok_or_else(|| "rbtdrc: no invocation context for charge".to_string())?;
101	        zrbtdrc_charge_impl(ctx)
102	    })
103	}
104

content.text:
Oriented. Filesystem unchanged. Here's the complete work plan:

## Current state — three pieces (one is already a tombstone)

- **`Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh:79`** — `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30`. Grep shows **no live bash consumer** — only the definition + a comment narrative. It's already orphaned; production sleep currently lives in Rust.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2121-2125`** — `pub const RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS: u64 = 30`. Real production source.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs:198-204`** — `zrbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` matcher (srjcl/pluml only).
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs:153-161`** — the post-charge wait block in `zrbtdrc_charge_impl`.

Forward-pointing comments at `rbtdrc_crucible.rs:85-94`, `:195-197`, `:2123-2124`, and `rbtdre_engine.rs:88` already name this pace as the migration target.

## Charge call chain
`tt/rbw-cC.Charge.{moniker}.sh` → launcher → `rbw_workbench.sh` → zipper entry `RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE` (imprint folio) → `rbob_charge` in `Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh:414-469`. The wait belongs at the tail of `rbob_charge`, after `buc_step "Crucible started"` (line 468).

## Nameplate fact: plumbing

**`Tools/rbk/rbrn_regime.sh` (`zrbrn_kindle`)** — add an enrollment line. `buv_decimal_enroll` with a 0–N bound matches the pattern used by `RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK`. Question: own group ("Service Readiness") or appended to "Container Image Configuration"? The field characterizes the bottle vessel's startup; either works. Lean toward a new one-field group since it's logically orthogonal to vessel identity.

**`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc`** — add a linked term following the `//axhrv_variable` + `{rbrn_xxx}` + prose pattern (template at lines 88–107). Update mapping section accordingly.

**Per-nameplate `.rbk/<moniker>/rbrn.env`** — five files exist; the docket names three:
- `tadmor` → 0
- `srjcl` → 30
- `pluml` → 30
- `moriah` → **0** — runtime-identical to tadmor per `RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH` comment at `rbtdrc_crucible.rs:2138`.
- `ccyolo` → **unknown**, need to confirm at execution time (not currently in the fixture matcher, so it inherits the matcher's `false` → 0 today; safest is 0).

## Excision

In `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`:
- Delete lines 153–161 (wait block in `zrbtdrc_charge_impl`).
- Delete `zrbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay` (195–204).
- Delete `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS` const + comment (2121–2125).
- Trim docstring on `rbtdrc_charge_crucible` (85–94) — drop the readiness-delay narrative and the BBAAt forward-pointer; charge setup is now just charge + CrucibleActive assertion.

In `rbtdre_engine.rs:88` — the comment lists `needs_readiness_delay` among **prior** side-channel matchers replaced by the Fixture struct. Historical-tense reading; leave alone. Re-confirm at edit-time.

In `rbcc_Constants.sh` — delete lines 74–79 (4-line preface comment + the const).

## Charge addition (rbob_bottle.sh tail)

After line 468:
```bash
if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
  buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for service readiness"
  sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
fi
```
No `buc_die` on missing — `zrbrn_kindle` enrollment guarantees the variable exists after regime load.

## Verification path
- `tt/vow-b.Build.sh` + `tt/vow-t.Test.sh` — Rust changes compile + unit tests green.
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` + `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh` — theurge build/tests.
- `tt/rbw-rnv.ValidateNameplateRegime.sh <each moniker>` — new field validates per nameplate.
- `tt/rbw-rnr.RenderNameplateRegime.sh tadmor` — render shows new field.
- Wrap criterion (per docket): `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.crucible.sh` — covers tadmor-security, srjcl-jupyter, pluml-diagram. This is the green-light gate.

## Risk surface
- **`ccyolo` value** — not in current matcher (i.e., today's wait = 0). Default-to-0 preserves behavior; flag at execution to confirm with operator.
- **Enrollment placement** — new group vs existing. Cosmetic; pick at edit-time, lean toward own group for orthogonality.
- **The "three colluding pieces" framing** — strictly two are load-bearing in production (Rust const + Rust matcher feeding the wait block). The bash const is already a tombstone — its removal is a sweep, not a collapse. Worth noting in the wrap commit so the docket's accounting is honest.

## Estimated change footprint
~5 files modified, ~3 nameplate env files appended, ~1 spec file extended. ~30 lines net deletion (excisions outweigh additions).

Ready to execute once the parallel chat releases the filesystem.

lines: Hallmark timestamp for the bottle container image.
+Service readiness configuration controls post-charge wait behavior for bottle services that need warmup time before responding to traffic.
+Seconds to wait after compose-up returns before the charge tabtarget completes.
+A fixed sleep, not a probe — generous values are cheap and reliable.
+Set to 0 when the bottle has no warmup phase (e.g., adversarial-only nameplates).

RBRN_MONIKER=ccyolo
RBRN_DESCRIPTION="Claude Code sandbox with SSH entry, Anthropic-only network access"
RBRN_RUNTIME=docker

# Vessel Source Configuration (GAR vessels)
RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=rbev-sentry-deb-tether
RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=rbev-bottle-ccyolo
RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=k260506180931-1b5b0fb5
RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=k260506180932-192abb91

# Entry Service Configuration (SSH from workstation into bottle)
RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=2222
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=22

# Network Uplink Configuration
RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=10000
RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=allowlist
RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=allowlist
# www.internic.net is a test target for verifying network containment — proves
# allowlist works for a non-Anthropic domain. ICANN-owned, 192.0.32.0/20.
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS="160.79.104.0/23 192.0.32.0/20"
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS="api.anthropic.com claude.ai platform.claude.com www.internic.net"

# Network Configuration
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=10.242.3.0
RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=24
RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=10.242.3.2
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=10.242.3.3

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Source RBBC bootstrap constants (source-time, before kindle)
test -n "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_CONFIG_DIR not set - rbcc requires launcher environment"
source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
RBCC_role_governor="governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
RBCC_role_payor="payor"
RBCC_role_assay="assay"
RBCC_role_mason="mason"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Cult-verb tinder — used by SA-management surface (invest/divest/roster) and
# composed into fact-extension constants below.
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_director}"
RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set - rbcc requires BURC environment"

  # Kindle constants (depend on runtime state)
  readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.text:
All five nameplates validate clean with the new field. Theurge builds and all 97 unit tests pass.

## Summary

Mechanical work complete:

**Added**
- `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` enrolled in `rbrn_regime.sh` under new "Service Readiness" group (decimal 0–300)
- Linked-term entry in `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc` + mapping section in `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc` (variable + group)
- Field set in all 5 nameplate env files: tadmor/moriah/ccyolo=0, srjcl/pluml=30
- Wait block appended to `rbob_charge` in `rbob_bottle.sh` — gated on `> 0`

**Removed**
- `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`: post-charge wait block (lines 153-161), `zrbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay()` matcher, `RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS` const, BBAAt forward-pointer in `rbtdrc_charge_crucible` docstring
- `rbcc_Constants.sh`: orphan `RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` and its 4-line comment

**Verified**
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` clean
- `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh` — 97/97 pass
- `tt/rbw-rnv.ValidateNameplateRegime.sh` clean for tadmor, srjcl, pluml, moriah, ccyolo
- No dangling references (single remaining mention is historical-tense in `rbtdre_engine.rs:88`)

**Note on docket framing**: the "three colluding pieces" framing was slightly overstated — the bash const was already orphaned (referenced only by a Rust comment), so the live production collusion was two-piece (Rust const + Rust matcher feeding the wait block). The bash removal was a tombstone sweep alongside.

**Wrap criterion** per docket says "tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution" — that's the crucible test suite. I have NOT run `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.crucible.sh` since it spins up real containers and takes time; that's your call when ready. The bujb_jurisdiction.sh staged change from another officium has been left untouched. Ready to notch when you say.

input.params: {"identity": "BBAAt", "files": ["Tools/rbk/rbrn_regime.sh", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc", "Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBS0-SpecTop.adoc", ".rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env", ".rbk/srjcl/rbrn.env", ".rbk/pluml/rbrn.env", ".rbk/moriah/rbrn.env", ".rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env", "Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs", "Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh"], "intent": "Fold post-charge readiness wait into the charge tabtarget, driven by a new per-nameplate fact RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC. Adds the field to rbrn_regime.sh under a new 'Service Readiness' group (decimal 0-300), plumbs the linked term through RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc and RBS0-SpecTop.adoc mapping section, and sets per-nameplate values: tadmor/moriah/ccyolo=0 (adversarial bottles and SSH-only entry need no warmup), srjcl/pluml=30 (Jupyter and PlantUML need warmup before responding). rbob_charge tail consults the fact and sleeps only when > 0; zrbrn_kindle enrollment guarantees the variable exists so no defensive check needed. Excises the theurge-side readiness machinery: the post-charge wait block in zrbtdrc_charge_impl, the zrbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay matcher, the RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS const, and the BBAAt forward-pointers in the rbtdrc_charge_crucible docstring. Also removes the orphaned bash const RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC in rbcc_Constants.sh (only referenced by a Rust comment — the production wait was Rust-side; bash side was a documentation tombstone). The docket's 'three colluding pieces' framing was thus slightly overstated: the live collusion was two-piece (Rust const + Rust matcher feeding the wait block), with the bash const as accompanying dead code that this pace also sweeps. Verified: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean, tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh passes 97/97, tt/rbw-rnv.ValidateNameplateRegime.sh clean for all five nameplates (tadmor, srjcl, pluml, moriah, ccyolo) with the new field showing PASS [decimal]. Crucible fixture suite not yet executed — that's the operator-decision verification gate. Single remaining mention of needs_readiness_delay is the historical-tense comment at rbtdre_engine.rs:88 about prior side-channel matchers replaced by the Fixture struct, which remains accurate."}

content.text:
Notched at `0a850d57`.

## What's next

**Wrap gate**: The docket's wrap criterion explicitly names "tadmor/srjcl/pluml fixtures pass under fixture execution." That means running `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.crucible.sh` (covers tadmor-security, srjcl-jupyter, pluml-diagram — 117 cases, spins real containers). Until that's green, the pace isn't truly done.

**Two options**:
1. Run the crucible suite now to close the loop, then wrap.
2. Defer suite run if a parallel chat or other constraint makes container-spinning awkward, and wrap on confidence (the readiness migration is mechanically faithful — `30 → 30`, `0 → 0` — and theurge unit tests cover the matcher excision).

I'd lean option 1 since the suite is the explicit wrap criterion and the heat (BB rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification) exists precisely to catch cross-section bugs that unit tests miss. Worth the wall time.

Which?

content.text:
Charge failed — **not a BBAAt regression**. The fixture's sentry hallmark in `.rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env` is `k260508182007-3b17a228` (k-prefix = local kludge), built on the linux side per commit `c076e346` (BBABE). On this mac, that image was never produced locally, so charge tries to summon from GAR and hits 403 (k-prefix kludges are local-only by construction).

Recent BBABE commits show the same cross-platform pattern — each machine re-kludges its sentry/bottle before charging.

**Mechanism**: nameplate hallmark mismatch with this host's local image cache, inherited from the parallel-station officium's most recent commit.

**Repair**: re-kludge sentry + bottle on this mac:
```
tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.tadmor.sh
tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.tadmor.sh
```
This rebuilds the kludges locally, writes new `k…` hallmarks into `.rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env`, then `tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh` should run clean.

But that **mutates the env file** — same file the parallel chat may be touching, and the staged `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` change hints another officium is active. Multi-officium discipline says don't fix what looks like another officium's work without asking.

Stop and ask: should I re-kludge on this mac (will rewrite `.rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env`), or defer the fixture run until the parallel chat coordinates?

Bottle kludge refuses on unstaged tree. Sentry kludge wrote the new hallmark into `.rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env` (unstaged). It's a pattern previously committed as BBABE-flavored work ("drive freshly-built kludge hallmark into nameplate"), but I'm doing it inside BBAAt's wrap verification.

Two reasonable framings for the commit:
1. **Heat-affiliated under BB** — "mac-side sentry kludge for tadmor — cross-platform fixture-run prep" (mirrors BBABE's recent commits).
2. **Pace-affiliated under BBAAt** — debatable; the env edit isn't migration work, it's environment plumbing.

I'd lean (1). The intent matches existing BBABE commits exactly. Want me to notch heat-affiliated under BB and then re-run the bottle kludge?

Added the field to rbrn_regime.sh under a new 'Service Readiness' group (decimal 0-300), plumbed the linked term through RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc and the RBS0-SpecTop.adoc mapping section, and set per-nameplate values: tadmor/moriah/ccyolo=0 (adversarial bottles and SSH-only entry need no warmup), srjcl/pluml=30 (Jupyter and PlantUML need warmup before responding). rbob_charge tail consults the fact and sleeps only when > 0; zrbrn_kindle enrollment guarantees the variable exists. Excised the theurge-side readiness machinery: the post-charge wait block in zrbtdrc_charge_impl, the zrbtdrc_needs_readiness_delay matcher, the RBTDRC_SERVICE_READINESS_DELAY_SECS const, and BBAAt forward-pointers in rbtdrc_charge_crucible's docstring. Also removed the orphaned bash const RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC (referenced only by a Rust comment — bash side was a documentation tombstone). Docket's 'three colluding pieces' framing was thus slightly overstated: the live collusion was two-piece (Rust const + Rust matcher feeding the wait block), with the bash const as accompanying dead code that this pace also swept. Verified: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean, tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 97/97 pass, tt/rbw-rnv.ValidateNameplateRegime.sh clean for all five nameplates with new field showing PASS [decimal], and tadmor fixture (tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh) ran 54/54 passed end-to-end exercising the zero-delay path through the new code. srjcl and pluml fixtures not run here (they exercise the non-zero path); operator decided tadmor evidence sufficient for wrap given the mechanically-faithful nature of the migration (30 → 30 preserved). Side cost: required mac-side sentry+bottle re-kludge for tadmor since prior hallmarks were linux-local k-prefix from BBABE; the two cross-platform re-kludge commits (c16de64a, d6d99638) landed under heat-affiliated notches per BBABE pattern."}
